project.name: Headhunt Manager (FDIWork)
project.type: Next.js 16 App Router, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL, TailwindCSS
project.status: CRM MVP complete, FDIWork core flows implemented, security hardening applied, candidate detail DB optimization implemented

features: Authentication (NextAuth.js + custom JWT for employer portal)
Candidate Management (tags, seniority, skills, multi-CV, languages, work history)
Job Management & Applicant Pipeline (notes, interview tracking)
FDIWork Public Website (homepage, jobs, companies, apply)
Employer Portal (register, dashboard, manage job postings)
CRM Integration (import FDIWork applications to CRM, employer-client linking)

database_schema.summary: ATS + employer portal + public job board
database_schema.relationships_explained: A Candidate can now have many CV files, many language entries, and many work experience records. Legacy Candidate.cvFileUrl and Candidate.cvFileName stay mirrored to the primary CV during the transition.
notable_constraints: Employer has unique email and unique clientId link

conventions: Use PostgreSQL String[] only for simple lists; use relation tables for structured candidate detail
Server Actions return success/error objects instead of throwing to the client
Admin protection must exist at route, UI, and server action layers
Candidate primary CV stays mirrored to legacy Candidate.cvFileUrl and Candidate.cvFileName during migration
security_notes: EMPLOYER_JWT_SECRET is required and has no default fallback
Basic rate limiting is applied to CRM login, employer auth, public upload, and public apply
xlsx was removed from the codebase because of a high severity advisory
